query: Feeling not good due to exams
response: I'm so sorry to hear that you're feeling stressed and overwhelmed with your upcoming exams. It's completely normal to feel that way, especially when there's a lot at stake.  First of all, take a deep breath and remember that it's okay to feel anxious. It doesn't mean you're not prepared or capable - it just means you're human!  Here are some suggestions that might help you cope with your exam anxiety:  1. **Take breaks**: Make sure you're giving yourself time to relax and recharge between study sessions. Go for a walk, do some yoga, or watch a funny video to take your mind off things. 2. **Prioritize self-care**: Get enough sleep, eat nutritious food, and stay hydrated. Exercise can also help reduce stress and improve focus. 3. **Reframe your thinking**: Try to focus on the positive aspects of this experience. You're learning new things, developing skills, and growing as a person. Remind yourself that you've got this! 4. **Make a plan**: Break down your study sessions into smaller chunks, and set specific goals for what you want to achieve each day. This can help you feel more in control. 5. **Seek support**: Talk to friends, family, or a trusted adult about how you're feeling. Sharing your concerns with someone who cares can help you feel heard and understood. 6. **Practice relaxation techniques**: Try activities like meditation, deep breathing, or progressive muscle relaxation to calm your mind and body. 7. **Reward yourself**: Give yourself something to look forward to after the exams are over - it could be a fun activity, a treat, or simply some well-deserved relaxation time.  Remember, you're not alone in this feeling! Many students face exam anxiety, and it's nothing to be ashamed of. You've got the power to manage your stress levels and come out on top!  Hang in there, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any further support or just someone to talk to.

Deep breathing exercises: Take slow, deep breaths, focusing on inhaling through the nose and exhaling slowly through the mouth. This can help calm the mind and reduce muscle tension. 2. Progressive muscle relaxation: Tense each muscle group in your body for a few seconds, then release the tension and move on to the next muscle group. 3. Visualization: Imagine yourself in a peaceful place, such as a beach or a forest. Focus on the sights, sounds, and smells of this place to help calm your mind. 4. Meditation: Sit quietly and focus on your breath for several minutes, letting go of any distracting thoughts. 5. Yoga: Practice gentle yoga poses to stretch and relax your muscles, while also focusing on your breathing. These techniques can be done at home or in a quiet location, making them easy to incorporate into your daily routine. They each do this technique once during a week, on different days (Monday to Friday) at different times (10am-11am, 11am-12pm, 12pm-1pm, 1pm-2pm, 2pm-3pm). From clue 6, the person practicing Yoga did so at 2 pm. From clue 3, the person meditated after the Progressive Muscle Relaxation which means neither of these can be practiced at 2 pm. Therefore, Yoga and Meditation cannot be on Tuesday or Wednesday according to clues 4 and 9. So they must be on Thursday or Friday.  From clue 8, Alice and Bob both did their relaxation techniques one day after the person who used Deep Breathing Exercises which means none of them can practice at 2 pm (the time for Yoga) and none of them can practice at 1pm as it's the earliest time left for Deep Breathing Exercises. So, Alice and Bob must have practiced on Tuesday or Wednesday.  From clue 7, the person who practiced Progressive Muscle Relaxation didn't do so on Tuesday or Wednesday which means they must've done it on Thursday since Friday is taken by Yoga from step 1. This implies Clara did her technique at 2 pm (Yoga) as per clue 6.  This leaves Alice and Bob to have practiced on Tuesday and Wednesday but Alice can't practice Meditation according to clue 1, so she must have practiced Deep Breathing Exercises in the morning (since it's the only one left for her). This means Bob did his technique at 11 am (the earliest time left) which is after Deep Breathing Exercises.  From clues 3 and 5, the person who meditated did so after the Progressive Muscle Relaxation (Tuesday) and before Visualization. So, David must have done his on Thursday as it's the only day left for him. And Alice can't do Meditation according to clue 1, so she must've visualized something at 10 am (the earliest time left).  This leaves Eve with Yoga. She couldn’t do it at 2 pm as it is taken by Clara and she also can’t do it on Monday or Friday because of clues 4 and 1 respectively, thus her Yoga session must have been at 12pm.
